india_trends.title: Domestic capital drives India's real estate boom as inflows jump 72% to $5.1 billion in Q1
india_trends.summary: India's real estate sector attracted $5.1 billion in institutional capital during the March quarter, a 72% year-on-year surge. Domestic investors dominated, accounting for 96% of inflows, with developers and REITs leading deployment. This highlights strong confidence in India's property market despi

india_trends.title: Tesla India expands charging network, service centres
india_trends.summary: Tesla is boosting its charging and service network across India. The company is adding Supercharger stations on major highways connecting key cities. This expansion aims to connect Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, and Chennai. Home charging is already available in 28 states. Tesla is building a comprehensi

india_trends.title: Jio Financial Services, Allianz Group ink 50:50 general, health insurance JV
india_trends.summary: Jio Financial Services and Allianz Group from Germany are joining forces in an exciting new insurance initiative. This equal partnership will concentrate on providing general and health insurance products uniquely designed for Indian consumers.

india_trends.title: PNB Housing Finance targets 18–20% loan growth, bets on affordable segment
india_trends.summary: PNB Housing Finance anticipates strong loan portfolio expansion of 18-20 percent this fiscal year. The company aims to reach a significant loan book of one lakh crore rupees by FY27. This growth will be fueled by a strategic focus on affordable and emerging market segments. india_trends.title: Andhra Pradesh to issue power distribution licences to large data centres
india_trends.summary: Andhra Pradesh is introducing a new policy to grant power distribution licenses to large data centers. This initiative aims to ensure a consistent and dependable electricity supply for these facilities. Companies meeting specific criteria can build and operate their own internal power distribution s

india_trends.title: Microdrama apps chase telco bundles as subscription growth lags despite download surge
india_trends.summary: Microdrama platforms are partnering with telecom operators to combat market fragmentation and boost subscriptions. india_trends.title: AI to reshape financial market infrastructure, says State Bank of India chairman CS Setty
india_trends.summary: State Bank of India chairman CS Setty foresees artificial intelligence revolutionizing financial markets. AI will enhance risk management and operational efficiency. india_trends.title: Clearing Corporation of India Ltd eyes AI-led, resilient market infra growth
india_trends.summary: Future financial markets require enhanced agility, intelligence, and integration. CCIL Chairman Rajeshwar Rao highlighted the need to manage cyber threats and supply chain disruptions. Emerging technologies like AI and machine learning will boost efficiency and resilience. CCIL aims to create a unif

india_trends.title: India allows ethanol blending in aviation fuel
india_trends.summary: India has allowed blending ethanol and synthetic hydrocarbons into aviation fuel. This move aims to cut emissions and reduce oil imports. While no immediate blending targets are set, India plans to blend 1 percent sustainable aviation fuel into international flights by 2027. This aligns with global

india_trends.title: Asus eyes India AI boom, in talks to supply high-end compute servers beyond PCs
india_trends.summary: Taiwanese tech giant Asus is expanding its India operations beyond PCs. The company is in discussions with the Indian government and major cloud providers to supply advanced AI compute servers. Asus already has a strong presence in the AI server market and partners with Yotta Data Services.

india_trends.title: Govt eyes ethanol blend beyond 20%, pushes flex-fuel vehicles
india_trends.summary: India is exploring higher ethanol blending in petrol and flex-fuel vehicles. This move aims to reduce reliance on imported oil. Discussions are underway with industry stakeholders. The government is preparing for increased biofuel use in transportation. This initiative seeks to boost domestic biofue

india_trends.summary: GLOBAL-FOREX:Dollar gains as Iran war keeps central banks in wait-and-see mode

india_trends.title: State capex hits 2.7% of GDP under SASCI scheme: Report
india_trends.summary: The SASCI scheme has boosted state capital spending to 2.7% of GDP. However, utilization of central loans for capital expenditure is uneven across states. Some states like Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra show strong uptake, while others like Kerala and Telangana lag. This divergence is attributed to

india_trends.title: Induction cooktop makers may be enticed with duty, GST cuts
india_trends.summary: India is weighing tax cuts on induction cooktops. This aims to boost local manufacturing and make electric cooking more affordable. The government is considering lowering customs duties on components. It also proposes reducing the GST on appliances. These measures address demand-supply challenges an

india_trends.title: BSNL CMD role may open to private sector as govt looks beyond internal candidates
india_trends.summary: The government is considering appointing a private sector professional as the next Chairman and Managing Director (CMD) of Bharat Sanchar Nigam Ltd (BSNL). This move aims to fill the leadership vacuum after the previous CMD's exit and follows delays in internal appointments. The decision is expected

india_trends.title: US stocks: FICO shares slide as Freddie Mac, Fannie Mae begin accepting rival VantageScore
india_trends.summary: FICO shares dropped 13% after Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae announced they will accept mortgages scored by rival VantageScore 4.0. This move aims to reduce homebuyer costs and increase competition in the credit scoring market. The housing giants will begin with a limited rollout to approved lenders.

india_trends.title: 'Disaster resilience must be built into infra projects,' says Economic affairs secretary Anuradha Thakur
india_trends.summary: Anuradha Thakur, Economic Affairs Secretary, emphasized integrating disaster resilience into infrastructure planning and funding. She stated that resilience is essential, not optional, and must be incorporated from the outset. Investing in resilient infrastructure upfront reduces long-term costs and

india_trends.title: India eyes 156 GW wind capacity by 2036: Pralhad Joshi
india_trends.summary: India is gearing up for a transformative leap in wind energy, aiming for an impressive 100 gigawatts by 2030 and an ambitious 156 gigawatts by 2036. This initiative reflects the nation’s commitment to a greener future, as it embarks on a clean energy journey.

india_trends.title: L&T arm signs green ammonia supply deal with Japan's ITOCHU
india_trends.summary: Larsen & Toubro's clean energy arm, LTEGL, has secured a long-term agreement with Japan's ITOCHU Corporation to supply 300,000 tonnes of green ammonia annually from its Kandla facility. This deal, building on a prior pact, aims to establish Kandla as a significant export hub for low-carbon fuels

india_trends.title: Finance Ministry plans to broaden Actuaries Act scope beyond insurance, pensions
india_trends.summary: The finance ministry is set to update the Actuaries Act, 2006. This will expand actuarial science to include data science and risk management. A new framework for regulating actuarial firms will also be introduced. These changes aim to modernize the profession and meet growing economic needs. The bi

world_trends.summary: Your insurer is betting you won’t appeal — but research shows 1 million denials could have been overturned if patients pushed back.

world_trends.summary: Ailing Spirit Airlines was hoping to emerge from bankruptcy in the next few months. The government may lend it a helping hand sooner.
